messages.content:
Sorry to hear you lost your debit card. I can help begin the bank’s fraud-investigation steps. Based only on the bank policy and your account data I have, here’s what I can do and what I need from you.

What I’ve already checked (per bank policy)
- We retrieved the account’s transactions for the last 7 days. The transactions on file are:
  - Amazon — 2 transactions of 2500 each on 2026-07-19
  - Swiggy — 480 on 2026-07-18
- The two identical Amazon postings may be duplicates or could be separate charges; timestamps and card-level details are not available in the data I have, so I cannot determine that yet.

Required next steps (policy-required)
1. Verify your identity using the bank’s approved authentication procedures. (I cannot perform any account actions — including blocking the card or issuing a replacement — until identity is verified.)
2. After authentication we will:
   - Confirm which transactions you did or did not authorize.
   - Complete the 7-day transaction review and try to identify any duplicate or suspicious charges.
   - Escalate immediately if there are indicators of identity theft, account takeover, international fraud, or wire fraud (these categories require immediate escalation under policy).

What I need from you now
- Confirm whether any of the transactions above are unauthorized.
- Tell me:
  - When and where you last had the card (date/time and location if possible).
  - Whether the card was lost, stolen, or simply misplaced.
  - Any other observations (possible fraudulent contacts, unknown SMS/calls about transactions, etc.).
- Please contact the bank immediately through your secure banking channel (secure message, phone number, or branch on file) so you can be authenticated and we can act. I don’t have your contact/channel details in the data provided.

Important notes and limitations
- I cannot block the card, reissue a card, or move money — those actions require you to be authenticated and must be done by bank staff following procedures. The retrieved policy requires identity verification before any account action.
- I cannot guarantee reimbursement for unauthorized charges — the policy explicitly says we must not guarantee reimbursement.
- The policy and data I have do not include:
  - The bank’s exact card-blocking or replacement procedure and timelines,
  - The authentication methods (specific KYC steps),
  - Transaction timestamps or full merchant descriptors needed to conclusively determine duplicate charges.
Because of those gaps, I can’t complete the investigation or make any determinations until you are authenticated and we obtain full transaction details.

If you want, tell me now whether the listed Amazon and Swiggy transactions are unauthorized and confirm you will contact the bank’s secure channel so I can outline the precise next steps they will perform after authentication. If you report the transactions unauthorized during authentication, the bank will proceed with the fraud investigation and escalate if appropriate.

plan.thought: Customer reports a lost debit card. To plan the investigation and remediation I need the bank's official lost/stolen card procedures (blocking, reissue, verification, liability and fraud escalation) plus the customer's profile (contact methods, verification requirements, account status) and recent transactions (to detect any unauthorized activity). With those inputs I can produce an ordered step-by-step execution plan (what to do immediately, what to verify, what to escalate, customer communications and follow-up).

steps.reason: Retrieve the bank's official procedures and policies for lost or stolen debit cards (card blocking/temporary lock, replacement/reissue process, customer verification/KYC steps, liability for unauthorized transactions, fraud monitoring/escalation and timeline requirements).

arguments.query: lost or stolen debit card procedure card blocking reissue customer verification liability unauthorized transactions fraud escalation timeline

steps.reason: Obtain the customer's profile to determine account type, contact channels on file, verification requirements, preferred branch/region, and any flags or prior alerts that affect how we handle the case.

Retrieve recent transactions on the customer's account to identify any potentially unauthorized activity since the card was lost and to determine whether immediate provisional credits or dispute/chargeback steps are needed.

tool_outputs.reason: Retrieve the bank's official procedures and policies for lost or stolen debit cards (card blocking/temporary lock, replacement/reissue process, customer verification/KYC steps, liability for unauthorized transactions, fraud monitoring/escalation and timeline requirements).

arguments.query: lost or stolen debit card procedure card blocking reissue customer verification liability unauthorized transactions fraud escalation timeline

tool_outputs.output:
Scope
Applies to debit cards, credit cards, ATM withdrawals and POS purchases.

Investigation Procedure

Step 1
Verify customer identity using approved authentication procedures.

Step 2
Retrieve all transactions for the reported account covering the previous seven calendar days.

Step 3
Identify transactions having:
- identical merchant
- identical amount
- identical card
- transaction timestamps within acceptable duplicate thresholds

Low Risk:
- Merchant clarification required

Escalation Matrix

Immediate escalation required for:
- Identity theft
- Account takeover
- International fraud
- Wire fraud

Compliance Notes

Never disclose investigation details.
Never accuse customers of fraud.
Never guarantee reimbursement.

Document ID: WF-OPS-002
Title: Fraud Investigation Policy
Version: 1.0

Purpose
This policy defines procedures for handling suspected fraudulent banking activity while minimizing financial loss and protecting customer accounts.

Scope
Applies to:
- Debit cards
- Credit cards
- Online Banking
- Wire Transfers
- Mobile Banking

Investigation Procedure

Authenticate customer identity.

Collect:
- Date
- Amount
- Merchant
- Channel
- Customer observations

tool_outputs.reason: Obtain the customer's profile to determine account type, contact channels on file, verification requirements, preferred branch/region, and any flags or prior alerts that affect how we handle the case.

Retrieve recent transactions on the customer's account to identify any potentially unauthorized activity since the card was lost and to determine whether immediate provisional credits or dispute/chargeback steps are needed.
